a1.name: reverify-on-activate
a1.bug_report: Bug report from a user: I recorded a memory linked to a file, then edited and committed that file. retrieve correctly flagged the memory as needs_review. I confirmed the lesson still holds and ran `roco status <id> activate`, which exited 0. But the very next retrieve flags the same memory needs_review again, forever. Marking a memory as re-verified seems to have no lasting effect. A related report says evidence-based auto-promotion from provisional to active has the same problem.
a1.retrieve_task_query: activate memory needs_review flag comes back after re-verify

a2.name: comment-only-reanchor
a2.bug_report: Bug report from a user: adding a single comment line (or a trailing blank line) to a Python file that a memory is linked to flags that memory needs_review on the next retrieve, even though nothing meaningful changed in the code. Cosmetic edits should not invalidate memories about .py files; the memory should stay healthy and follow the file's new version.
a2.retrieve_task_query: comment whitespace only edit python file flags memory needs_review spurious

a3.name: empty-repo-clean-errors
a3.bug_report: Bug report from a user: on a freshly `git init`-ed repository with zero commits, `roco retrieve` and `roco affected` crash with a raw Python traceback (CalledProcessError, exit 128 from git) instead of a clean one-line error message. Similarly, `roco merge` with a missing input file dumps a full traceback. CLI commands should never leak raw tracebacks for expected failure conditions.
a3.retrieve_task_query: roco retrieve crash traceback repo without commits git init clean error
